---
title: "Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates"
chapter_id: "semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-27"
primary_sources:
  - "vcm_public"
  - "verification_bandwidth"
  - "spinoza"
  - "context_engineer"
  - "vcm_editable"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Four local raw-cache mappings are passage-reviewed; `vcm_editable` remains connector/source-note mapped, and support remains argument until paired source/derived cells, summary-fidelity tests, or certificate truthfulness checks exist."
  - "Only protocol schema/fixture validation is implemented; the fixture now records transaction links, artifact links, revocation state, and non-claims, but no summary-fidelity or authority-preservation behavior test has been run for this chapter."
  - "Lean coverage is limited to finite certificate and source/summary authority records; summary fidelity and truthful-certificate checks remain unimplemented."
  - "No support-state promotion is implied by this drafting pass."
---

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-27 |
| Primary source records | `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`, `vcm_editable` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `vcm_public`; supporting: `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`; connector/recovery: `vcm_editable` |
| Source loading state | source notes: `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`, `vcm_editable`; passage-reviewed local raw cache: `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`; connector/source-note only: `vcm_editable` |
| Test state | Semantic page certificate fixture validation now includes transaction refs, artifact refs, revocation state, and non-claims; `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates` finite-record proofs are implemented; summary-fidelity tests remain planned. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter treats summaries and context cells as evidence-bounded representations. The repo validates a semantic page certificate fixture and implements narrow finite-record Lean predicates for derived-cell certificate completeness and source-to-summary authority non-escalation. It does not yet test summary fidelity, omission detection, certificate truthfulness, or support-state promotion from certificates.

It follows the ABI chapter by moving inside the materialized packet. Once context can be addressed and faulted, the next question is what each compact unit is allowed to mean.

The certificate is the border control for meaning. It prevents a compact cell from changing role as it moves from planning to evidence, execution, review, or memory.

::: {.asi-human-only}
## Human Reading Path

The Virtual Context ABI says how context enters the working surface. This chapter asks what the pieces of that context are allowed to mean. Semantic pages and context cells are compact units with provenance, loss boundaries, permitted uses, confidence limits, and certificates attached.

For the reader, the key idea is that summaries are not free. A summary can be useful only if the system records what it preserved, what it compressed away, and which downstream claims may rely on it. Otherwise compact context becomes a quiet source of evidence loss and accidental authority.
:::

## Problem

Compiled context needs typed semantic units and proof-carrying summaries that preserve provenance and loss contracts.

The ABI says how context is requested and materialized. This chapter says what the materialized units are. VCM should not treat every context object as interchangeable text. It should distinguish claims, constraints, decisions, corrections, events, artifacts, source excerpts, summaries, and derived cells.

Each derived representation needs a certificate: where it came from, what it omits, what authority labels survived, what it can be used for, and what kind of verification it can support. Without that certificate, a summary becomes a trust-me blob.

The same source may need multiple honest representations. A quote-preserving evidence cell, a terse planning brief, a redacted execution instruction, and a contradiction-checking pair can all derive from the same source, but they do not carry the same authority or verification value.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Summaries can look authoritative while dropping source bindings, omissions, authority ceilings, or allowed-use limits.

Summarization is not neutral. It can compress away dissent, uncertainty, chronology, exact wording, revocation state, or the fact that a source was only usable under a narrow authority label. Verification Bandwidth adds the reason this matters: a compact summary can make generation easier while making joint verification harder because the lost constraints are no longer available.

The fix is not to ban summaries. It is to make representation loss visible. A summary can be useful for planning, unsafe for citation, inadequate for proof, and forbidden for execution all at the same time.

A second insufficiency is role drift. A cell created as a planning summary can later be treated as evidence because it sounds precise. A cell created for evidence can later be sent to a worker who lacks clearance. Certificates should keep allowed use attached to the representation, not to the surrounding prose.

## Core Claim

[semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] VCM should represent context as typed semantic pages or cells with evidence-carrying representation certificates.

The claim remains at `argument` support. VCM public v1 supplies semantic objects, representations, validations, packets, materializations, and authority binding. Verification Bandwidth explains why compression loss must be visible. Spinoza supplies claim/evidence cell pressure. Context Engineer supplies mission-brief and need-to-know context pressure. The editable VCM source is a terminology refinement route, not a local certificate system.

### Claim-source mapping status

Appendix C maps this semantic-page claim to all assigned source notes. Four local raw-cache mappings are now passage-reviewed. The mappings support typed context cells, representation certificates, proof/citation/procedure-carrying claim records, and structured mission briefs, not summary fidelity, omission completeness, certificate truthfulness, open-domain formalization, or support-state promotion from certificates. `vcm_editable` remains connector/source-note mapped until durable raw text, conformance artifacts, certificate checker results, benchmark records, or external corroboration are imported or inspected.

| Source | What it supports | Limit |
|---|---|---|
| `vcm_public` | Passage-reviewed: context cells, semantic pages, stable logical addresses, page state fields, task-relative representation graphs, representation certificates, exact-decode versus semantic-materialization boundaries, future-query/strict-summary limits, use-relative sufficiency, authority non-escalation, and evidence/authority separation. | No end-to-end model superiority, summary-fidelity checker, certificate truthfulness run, or independent interoperability result exists here. |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Passage-reviewed: semantic-unit definitions, effective verification workspace, coherence as constraint satisfaction, pairwise grinding, the two-body verification limit, lossy summary pressure, interface-verification tradeoffs, hierarchical summary fidelity tradeoffs, contradiction-rate test design, and RAG-as-engineering-compromise framing. | The proposed verification, contradiction-rate, and summary-fidelity tests have not been run or mechanized here. |
| `spinoza` | Passage-reviewed: proof/citation/procedure-carrying belief graphs, proposition-node metadata, justification edges, tier labels, bounded formal scope, proof/citation/procedure artifacts, dependency tracing, contradiction revision, tier non-upgrade rules, semantic-isolation risk controls, and audit-trail expectations. | Does not prove open-domain formalization, arbitrary theorem validity, certificate truthfulness, or whole-system epistemic correctness. |
| `context_engineer` | Passage-reviewed: context supply chains, need-to-know delivery, governor placement, layered memory, briefer/mission-brief compression, context-as-code fields, clearance levels, context shards, memory masks, allowed tools, Digital SCIF lifecycle, zeroization, sanitized commits, and information-flow controls. | Synthetic hallucination, leak, cost, and latency claims are source-reported only; no leak, hallucination, cost, latency, or summary-fidelity harness is reproduced. |
| `vcm_editable` | Connector/source-note mapped: task-relative representation graphs and evidence-carrying certificates refine planner-guided context compilation, authority non-escalation, protected compilation, mounts, snapshots, and materializations. | Connector-readable refinement only; no VCM-Bench, certificate checker, or model-facing result has been run here. |

## Mechanism

Once the ABI can materialize a packet, the stack still needs to know what kind of context each unit is and what it lost during compression. VCM public v1 supplies semantic objects, representations, validations, packets, materializations, protected compilation, and authority binding. Verification Bandwidth supplies the warning that summary convenience can erase verification capacity. Spinoza supplies claim/evidence cell discipline, Context Engineer supplies scoped mission briefs and clearance constraints, and editable VCM sharpens task-relative representation graphs and evidence-carrying certificates.

A Semantic Page Certificate is the representation receipt that lets a compact cell remain useful without pretending to be raw evidence.

The reviewed passages sharpen the chapter's boundary. VCM public v1 justifies typed pages, representation certificates, future-query limits, and authority non-escalation. Verification Bandwidth justifies recording summary loss as a verification constraint. Spinoza justifies treating claim/evidence cells as graph records with proof, citation, procedure, tier, dependency, and revision metadata. Context Engineer justifies structured mission briefs with clearance and memory-mask fields. None of those passages show this repository checking summary fidelity, proving certificate truthfulness, validating omission completeness, solving open-domain autoformalization, or reproducing source-reported benchmark figures.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Source cells"] --> B["Derived page / cell"]
  B --> C["Context transaction ref"]
  C --> D["Cell type"]
  D --> E["Source bindings"]
  E --> F["Omissions + loss contract"]
  F --> G["Authority ceiling"]
  G --> H["Permitted uses + revocation state"]
  H --> I{"Verification adequate?"}
  I -- "yes" --> J["Context packet or artifact ref"]
  I -- "no" --> K["Residual / stronger representation"]
```

The certificate layer classifies pages as constraints, claims, decisions, corrections, events, artifacts, source excerpts, summaries, or other typed cells. It then attaches source bindings, derivation links, omissions, loss contracts, validity windows, authority ceilings, permitted uses, verification states, and residual risks. Task-relative representation graphs replace a single compression ladder, so the same source can become different representations for planning, evidence, execution, or contradiction review without pretending all representations carry the same authority.

For example, the same source can produce a terse planning brief, a quotation-preserving evidence cell, a redacted execution packet, and a contradiction-checking pair. The certificate tells downstream layers which representation they are holding and which transaction or artifact record is responsible for it.

Spinoza consumes claim/evidence cells; planning consumes constraints and decisions; execution receives only permitted representations. The same cell should not silently change role when moved between layers.

Certificate updates should be explicit. If a source is revoked, a summary is corrected, an omission becomes material, or a representation is promoted from planning-only to evidence-capable, the certificate changes and the old one remains auditable. The update should leave a transaction reference, an artifact reference when a concrete work product exists, a revocation state, and explicit non-claims so later consumers cannot mistake the cell for raw source evidence.

## Interfaces

The interface is the Semantic Page Certificate.

- Spinoza consumes claim/evidence cells.
- Planning consumes constraints and decisions.
- Execution receives only permitted representations.

Minimum fields:

- `page_id`
- `cell_type`
- `source_bindings`
- `derived_from`
- `transaction_refs`
- `artifact_refs`
- `representation_kind`
- `loss_contract`
- `omissions`
- `authority_ceiling`
- `permitted_uses`
- `validity_window`
- `verification_state`
- `revocation_state`
- `residual_risks`
- `non_claims`

The certificate is what lets a compact cell remain honest. It says what the cell is, which sources bind it, which transaction created or updated it, which artifact carries it, what was lost, which authority ceiling survived, which uses are permitted, whether verification is adequate, what revocation state applies, and what residual risks remain.

The interface should distinguish exact carriage, lossy summary, redaction, abstraction, translation, and derived inference. Each representation kind has different failure modes and different permitted uses. Calling them all "context" erases the reason for the certificate.

## Invariants

- Authority labels survive summarization.
- Loss contracts are explicit.
- A derived cell points back to source bindings.
- A derived cell points back to the transaction or artifact record that created the representation when that record exists.

Authority cannot increase through summarization. A derived cell cannot become more citable, more executable, more secret-cleared, or more verified than the source material and certificate permit. If the certificate is missing, the cell is not eligible for support-state promotion.

Another invariant is use preservation under transfer. Moving a cell into a plan, claim ledger, job, report, or reader edition cannot broaden its permitted use. If the target use is outside the certificate, the system needs a stronger representation or a new review.

## Failure modes

- Summary overconfidence.
- Provenance loss.
- Authority escalation via compression.

Summary overconfidence happens when fluent compression hides the actual loss contract. Provenance loss breaks source-to-claim mapping. Authority escalation via compression happens when a summary strips labels and then looks safe to use elsewhere. Each failure should block the representation from being used as evidence.

The subtle failure is certificate laundering. A certificate exists, so downstream layers stop asking whether its fields are truthful, current, and adequate for the new use. The certificate should make those questions easier, not exempt the cell from them.

## Minimal implementation

A semantic-page schema with certificate fields and example source-to-summary derivation.

The smallest useful implementation is a `semantic_page_certificate` schema and fixture. It validates source bindings, derivation, transaction refs, artifact refs, representation kind, loss contract, omissions, authority ceiling, permitted uses, validity, verification state, revocation state, residual risks, and non-claims. The accompanying Lean module is intentionally narrower than a summary evaluator: it models finite certificate records, source cells, and summary cells; a valid derived cell must declare source bindings, a loss contract, and permitted uses; and a summary that respects its source cells cannot raise its authority ceiling above any source.

That validation proves only that the certificate names source bindings, loss, permitted use, and residual risk. The Lean predicates do not prove that the summary preserved the right facts, that omissions are complete, or that the certificate is truthful.

The next useful implementation step is a paired-cell fixture set: one source excerpt, one transaction record, one planning summary with loss recorded, one evidence cell preserving exact wording, one redacted execution cell with a lower authority ceiling, one stale certificate invalidated by source revocation, one artifact node that carries the derived cell, and one attempted use outside the certificate blocked. That would test certificate semantics without claiming summary fidelity.

## Beyond the State of the Art

The mature version is a representation-certification system for context. It does not merely summarize sources. It turns raw sources, exact excerpts, planning briefs, redacted instructions, claim cells, contradiction pairs, and derived inferences into typed objects whose permitted use, loss, authority, and provenance remain attached wherever the object travels.

At that end state, the same source can produce several honest cells: an exact evidence excerpt, a lossy planning brief, a redacted execution packet, a contradiction-checking pair, and a reader-edition paraphrase. Each cell carries a certificate saying what it is, what it came from, what was omitted, which transaction created it, which artifact carries it, what authority ceiling survived, what use is permitted, and what residual risk remains.

The final product surface would include:

- A typed semantic-page model for claims, constraints, decisions, corrections, events, artifacts, source excerpts, summaries, and derived cells.
- Representation certificates for exact carriage, lossy summary, redaction, abstraction, translation, and derived inference.
- Certificate lifecycle records for creation, update, revocation, stale use, blocked use, and source-driven correction.
- Use gates that prevent planning summaries from becoming citations, evidence excerpts from leaking into unauthorized workers, or reader paraphrases from becoming proof support.
- Certificate checkers that compare source cells and derived cells before support-state promotion, publication, or execution use.

For this chapter, beyond the state of the art means that compact context becomes safer as it moves, because every representation carries its own boundary conditions. This remains a target architecture. The support state should not rise until paired source/derived cells, certificate truthfulness checks, summary-fidelity tests, omission audits, and downstream use gates exist for this end state.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Semantic page certificate fixture validation | Check that the certificate fixture matches the public schema and declares bindings, derivation, transaction refs, artifact refs, representation kind, loss contract, omissions, authority, permitted uses, validity, verification state, revocation state, residual risks, and non-claims. | implemented by protocol validation; run in this pass |
| Derived-cell certificate predicate | Check that valid derived cells carry source bindings, a loss contract, and permitted uses. | implemented in `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates`; builds locally |
| Authority preservation predicate | Check that summaries respecting their sources cannot increase the authority ceiling of source cells. | implemented in `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates`; builds locally |
| Summary fidelity test | Check that omissions and exactness limits remain visible before evidence use. | planned; not run |
| Certificate truthfulness test | Check that certificate declarations match paired source and derived cells. | planned; not run |

The fixture-shape check and finite-record proof predicates are implemented. Fidelity, omission completeness, and certificate truthfulness require paired source/derived cells and an evaluator that can reject unsupported representations.

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:vcm.certificates.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates` | A derived context cell carries source bindings and a declared loss/use contract. | implemented |
| `lean:vcm.certificates.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates` | A summary cannot increase the authority ceiling of its source cells. | implemented |

These proof hooks are implemented only over finite Lean certificate and authority records. `AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates` checks that valid derived cells declare source bindings plus loss/use contracts, and that a summary respecting source authority cannot exceed any source ceiling. The proof does not evaluate summary fidelity, omission completeness, or certificate truthfulness.

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Layer | Planned use | Readiness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `vcm_public` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1 | memory_context | Public VCM release. Governed protocol for compiled working context. | source note available; local raw cache passage-reviewed |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts | context_verification_theory | Generation vs verification; long context is not automatically long-range reasoning. | source note available; local raw cache passage-reviewed |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | reasoning_epistemology | Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. | source note available; local raw cache passage-reviewed |
| `context_engineer` | Context Engineer / Manhattan Protocol | memory_context_lineage | Context as supply chain; compartmentalized information logistics. | source note available; local raw cache passage-reviewed |
| `vcm_editable` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1.0_Editable | memory_context | Editable version with evidence-carrying planner-guided context compiler framing. | source note available; connector or recovery required |

The crosswalk identifies sources for semantic context units and certificate discipline. Four mappings have passage review, but the core claim stays at `argument` because `vcm_editable` remains connector/source-note mapped and summary fidelity, omission completeness, certificate truthfulness, open-domain formalization, and benchmark claims remain unpromoted.

## Summary

Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates makes context units typed and evidence-carrying. The system can compress and adapt context, but it must keep provenance, loss, authority, permitted use, verification state, and residual risks attached.

This chapter is the antidote to overconfident summaries. A representation can be small, useful, and still honest about what it is not allowed to prove. The next chapter gives those certified units runtime semantics: snapshots, mounts, branches, taint, deletion closure, and auditable memory faults.

The practical standard is that a certified cell should become harder to misuse as it travels. If the certificate disappears into surrounding prose, the representation has become another unbounded summary.
